DPS-01.name: DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency)
DPS-01.description: Musk-led White House task force that executes agency workforce reductions, IT system audits, and spending freezes to centralize executive control over the bureaucracy.

DPS-01.name: White House Domestic Policy Council / Stephen Miller Policy Shop
DPS-01.description: The Miller-run policy apparatus that drafts executive orders, manages loyalty-based personnel installation across agencies, and coordinates Schedule F implementation.

DPS-01.name: Heritage Foundation / Project 2025 Implementation Network
DPS-01.description: Kevin Roberts's policy-and-personnel pipeline that pre-vetted political appointees, supplies agency-level playbooks, and provides ideological backbone for administrative-state dismantlement.

DPS-01.name: House Judiciary Committee (Majority)
DPS-01.description: Jim Jordan's committee provides legislative air cover, conducts investigations that justify firings and restructuring, and blocks oversight of executive consolidation moves.

DPS-01.name: Judicial Watch / Tom Fitton FOIA Operation
DPS-01.description: FOIA-litigation and public-pressure organization that surfaces bureaucratic resistance narratives, giving the White House pretexts for purges and restructuring.

DPS-02.name: Supreme Court Conservative Supermajority (Thomas-Alito-Gorsuch-Kavanaugh-Barrett bloc)
DPS-02.description: The six-justice bloc that issues major-questions, non-delegation, and Chevron-dismantling rulings that structurally transfer regulatory power from agencies to courts and Congress.

DPS-02.name: Federalist Society / Leonard Leo Judicial Network
DPS-02.description: The interlocking apparatus of the Federalist Society, Leo's Concord Fund, and Judicial Crisis Network that selects, funds, and coordinates the pipeline of judges and strategic litigation vehicles.

DPS-02.name: State Attorney General Republican Alliance (RAGA litigation coalition)
DPS-02.description: The coordinated bloc of Republican state AGs that files and joins challenges to federal regulations, generating the cases the Supreme Court uses to dismantle agency authority.

DPS-02.name: Senate Judiciary Committee (Republican majority under Graham)
DPS-02.description: Confirms Federalist Society pipeline judges at speed, holds hearings that delegitimize agency rulemaking, and blocks Democratic judicial nominees to maintain the supermajority's lower-court feeder system.

DPS-02.name: America First Legal Foundation
DPS-02.description: Stephen Miller's litigation arm that files targeted lawsuits against Biden-era and surviving administrative-state regulations, serving as a rapid-deployment complement to state AG challenges.

DPS-03.name: House Republican Leadership (Speaker Johnson / Scalise / Cole)
DPS-03.description: The Speaker's team that sets the floor calendar, negotiates continuing resolutions, manages the debt ceiling, and brokers between the Freedom Caucus and moderates to pass must-pass fiscal legislation.

DPS-03.name: House Freedom Caucus
DPS-03.description: The hard-right bloc that uses shutdown threats and procedural hostage-taking to extract deeper spending cuts, policy riders, and concessions from leadership on every fiscal vehicle.

DPS-03.name: Senate Republican Conference Leadership (Thune / Barrasso)
DPS-03.description: Manages floor procedure, whips 60-vote thresholds, sequences confirmations, and negotiates bipartisan deals on debt ceilings and appropriations with Schumer's caucus.

DPS-03.name: Senate Democratic Caucus (Schumer / Durbin leadership)
DPS-03.description: Uses filibuster leverage, amendment votes, and confirmation holds to extract concessions, block riders, and force bipartisan negotiations on fiscal deadlines.

DPS-03.name: House Ways and Means / Senate Finance Committees (Smith / Crapo)
DPS-03.description: The tax-writing committees that draft reconciliation bills, structure tariff authorities, and control the revenue side of every fiscal-brinkmanship negotiation.

DPS-04.name: OpenAI–Microsoft Axis
DPS-04.description: The Sam Altman–Satya Nadella partnership that combines frontier model development (GPT series) with Azure cloud distribution, forming the highest-capitalized integrated AI stack.

DPS-04.name: Google DeepMind / Alphabet AI Division
DPS-04.description: The merged Pichai-Hassabis AI unit that controls Gemini model development, TPU chip design, Google Cloud distribution, and the world's largest proprietary training-data corpus.

DPS-04.name: NVIDIA Compute Platform
DPS-04.description: Jensen Huang's GPU monopoly that sets the clock speed of the entire AI race by controlling the design, allocation, and pricing of H100/B200 training and inference accelerators.

DPS-04.name: Anthropic–Amazon Web Services Partnership
DPS-04.description: The Amodei siblings' safety-oriented frontier lab backed by Amazon's multi-billion-dollar investment and AWS distribution, positioning as the enterprise-trust alternative to OpenAI.

DPS-04.name: Meta FAIR / Llama Open-Weight Ecosystem
DPS-04.description: Zuckerberg's open-weight model strategy (Llama series) that commoditizes the model layer, shifts value to Meta's social-platform deployment, and disrupts closed-model pricing power.

DPS-05.name: Federal Reserve Board of Governors / FOMC
DPS-05.description: Powell-led monetary authority whose rate decisions, balance-sheet management, and bank supervision set the baseline interest-rate regime against which all fiscal and financial actors must operate.

DPS-05.name: Treasury Department (Bessent regime)
DPS-05.description: Scott Bessent's Treasury manages debt issuance strategy, coupon-vs-bill mix, tariff revenue flows, and sanctions policy, serving as the executive branch's primary interface with bond markets.

DPS-05.name: Senate Banking Committee (Crapo / Warren)
DPS-05.description: The committee that writes banking, crypto, and financial-regulation legislation, confirms financial regulators, and stages the partisan confrontation over Fed independence and stablecoin frameworks.

DPS-05.name: Coinbase / Crypto Industry Lobby (Stand With Crypto / Blockchain Association)
DPS-05.description: Brian Armstrong–led industry coalition that funds congressional campaigns, drafts stablecoin and market-structure bills, and pushes for CFTC-over-SEC jurisdiction to integrate crypto into the financial regime.

DPS-05.name: Macro Hedge Fund / Private Equity Nexus (Citadel, Apollo, Druckenmiller)
DPS-05.description: The Griffin–Rowan–Druckenmiller cluster of systemically significant capital allocators whose Treasury positioning, credit provision, and public commentary discipline or destabilize fiscal-monetary coordination.

DPS-06.name: Anduril Industries
DPS-06.description: Palmer Luckey and Trae Stephens's autonomy-and-software defense firm that builds Lattice, counter-drone systems, and autonomous vehicles, serving as the flagship disruptor of legacy prime contracting.

DPS-06.name: Palantir Technologies
DPS-06.description: Alex Karp's data-integration and AI platform that embeds across Army, IC, and combatant commands, positioning software-defined C2 as the new center of defense procurement.

DPS-06.name: SpaceX / Starshield
DPS-06.description: Musk's launch and satellite-communications monopoly whose Starshield division provides classified space services, making SpaceX a de facto space-domain prime contractor rivaling Lockheed and Northrop.

DPS-06.name: Scale AI / Defense Tech Software Cluster
DPS-06.description: Alexandr Wang's AI data-labeling and model-evaluation firm that anchors a broader cluster of software-native defense startups competing for CDAO, SOCOM, and service-branch AI contracts.

DPS-06.name: Senate Armed Services Committee (Wicker / Reed)
DPS-06.description: The bipartisan committee that writes the NDAA, sets procurement authorization levels, and decides whether acquisition reform language favors new entrants or protects legacy prime workshare.

DPS-07.name: X (Twitter) / Musk Platform Operations
DPS-07.description: Musk's ownership of X gives him direct control over algorithmic amplification, content-moderation policy, and political speech norms for the platform most used by journalists and political actors.

DPS-07.name: Fox News / Murdoch Media Empire
DPS-07.description: The Murdoch family's Fox News, Fox Business, and New York Post constitute the dominant right-of-center broadcast-and-print ecosystem that sets daily narrative frames for the Republican base.

DPS-07.name: Turning Point USA / Kirk–Rufo Conservative Media Network
DPS-07.description: Charlie Kirk's campus-and-digital organization combined with Christopher Rufo's issue-framing operation that manufactures and distributes culture-war narratives across social media and conservative outlets.

DPS-07.name: Meta Platforms (Facebook / Instagram / Threads)
DPS-07.description: Zuckerberg's platforms control the largest algorithmic news-distribution surface in the US; his content-moderation and fact-checking policy shifts directly reshape political information flow for billions.

DPS-07.name: Podcast–Influencer Ecosystem (Joe Rogan / Tucker Carlson Network)
DPS-07.description: The Rogan–Carlson axis of long-form independent media that commands massive audiences outside legacy editorial gatekeeping, increasingly serving as kingmaker interview venues for political figures.

DPS-08.name: American Petroleum Institute / Continental Resources (Hamm) / Koch Industries
DPS-08.description: The fossil-fuel trade association and its anchor members that lobby for accelerated drilling permits, LNG export approvals, and rollback of EPA emissions rules.

DPS-08.name: Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee (Barrasso / Murkowski)
DPS-08.description: The committee that writes permitting-reform legislation, controls federal land leasing policy, and negotiates the bipartisan deals that determine grid-buildout and energy-project timelines.

DPS-08.name: Hyperscaler Energy Procurement Bloc (Amazon / Google / Microsoft / Meta)
DPS-08.description: The coordinated push by cloud giants to secure gigawatts of firm power—nuclear PPAs, natural-gas peakers, and behind-the-meter generation—to feed AI data-center expansion.

DPS-08.name: Tesla Energy / xAI Power Operations
DPS-08.description: Musk's integrated energy play spanning Tesla Megapack grid storage, Tesla Energy retail, and xAI's direct power-procurement for its Memphis and other data-center clusters.

DPS-08.name: Earthjustice / Sierra Club Litigation Coalition
DPS-08.description: Abigail Dillen's Earthjustice and allied green groups that use NEPA and Clean Air Act litigation to block or delay fossil-fuel permits and force environmental review of data-center power demand.

DPS-09.name: Thiel Network (Founders Fund / Palantir / Anduril / allied ventures)
DPS-09.description: Peter Thiel's interlocking investment portfolio, board seats, and political patronage network that converts defense and intelligence contracts into policy influence and vice versa.

DPS-09.name: Musk Enterprise Cluster (Tesla / SpaceX / xAI / X / Neuralink / DOGE role)
DPS-09.description: The constellation of Musk-controlled companies whose simultaneous dependence on government contracts, regulatory forbearance, and political access makes Musk the paradigm case of techno-oligarchic entanglement.

DPS-09.name: Oracle / Ellison–Trump Alliance
DPS-09.description: Larry Ellison's Oracle leverages a close personal relationship with Trump to secure major government cloud contracts (CIA, DoD, TikTok deal structure) and AI infrastructure partnerships.

DPS-09.name: Ballard Partners / K Street Tech Lobby Nexus
DPS-09.description: Brian Ballard's lobbying firm serves as the primary transactional bridge between Silicon Valley executives seeking White House access and the Trump political operation seeking donor capital and platform cooperation.

DPS-09.name: Blackstone / Schwarzman Political-Capital Exchange
DPS-09.description: Stephen Schwarzman's Blackstone deploys infrastructure investment commitments (data centers, energy) and fundraising bundling in exchange for regulatory and tax treatment favorable to private equity and alternative assets.

DPS-10.name: ACLU / Democracy Defense Legal Coalition
DPS-10.description: Anthony Romero's ACLU anchors a coalition of voting-rights litigators (including NAACP LDF, Brennan Center) that files federal and state challenges to voter-roll purges, ID laws, and post-election dispute procedures.

DPS-10.name: Marc Elias / Democracy Docket Litigation Network
DPS-10.description: Elias's dedicated election-law firm and media platform that coordinates pre-election ballot-access litigation, post-election recount strategy, and real-time legal rapid-response across all 50 states.

DPS-10.name: Honest Elections Project / Cleta Mitchell Election Integrity Network
DPS-10.description: The Leo-funded Honest Elections Project and Mitchell's volunteer-training network that installs poll watchers, challenges voter registrations, and builds the legal and procedural infrastructure for contesting election results.

DPS-10.name: Faith & Freedom Coalition / Ralph Reed Voter Mobilization Machine
DPS-10.description: Reed's evangelical turnout operation that registers voters, distributes voter guides, and deploys church-network canvassing to maximize conservative base participation in battleground states.

DPS-10.name: Arabella Advisors / Soros-backed Civic Engagement Network (Sixteen Thirty Fund, New Venture Fund)
DPS-10.description: The progressive dark-money fiscal-sponsorship network that funds voter-registration drives, ballot-initiative campaigns, and counter-mobilization infrastructure through its constellation of pass-through entities.

DPS-01.name: White House Office (EOP: Chief of Staff + OMB + White House Counsel)
DPS-01.description: Centralizes operational control over agencies via staffing, budget apportionment leverage, legal sign-off, and executive-order implementation.

DPS-01.name: Office of Presidential Personnel (Presidential Personnel Office, PPO)
DPS-01.description: Installs loyalty-aligned political appointees and coordinates removals to reshape agency leadership and enforcement posture.

DPS-01.name: Office of Management and Budget (OMB) — Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA)
DPS-01.description: Uses regulatory review, guidance, and budgetary tools to steer or stall agency rulemaking and consolidate White House direction.

DPS-01.name: U.S. Office of Special Counsel (OSC)
DPS-01.description: Becomes a pivotal node in civil-service discipline/whistleblower pathways that can be tightened or redirected during workforce restructuring.

DPS-01.name: Department of Justice (DOJ) — Office of the Attorney General
DPS-01.description: Shapes selective enforcement and legal posture across the executive branch through prosecutorial discretion, opinions, and litigation strategy.

DPS-02.name: Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS)
DPS-02.description: Issues doctrinal rulings narrowing agency deference and expanding judicial control over regulatory interpretation and enforcement limits.

DPS-02.name: Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies
DPS-02.description: Maintains the conservative judicial pipeline and networks legal theory, vetting, and placement for administrative-state limiting jurisprudence.

DPS-02.name: Judicial Crisis Network
DPS-02.description: Finances and coordinates confirmation campaigns and messaging that protect and extend the conservative court majority.

DPS-02.name: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit
DPS-02.description: Functions as a high-impact venue for administrative-state challenges through injunctions and precedents that tee up Supreme Court review.

DPS-02.name: Alliance Defending Freedom (ADF)
DPS-02.description: Runs strategic litigation that tests and expands doctrines constraining agency authority and federal regulatory reach.

DPS-03.name: House Committee on Rules
DPS-03.description: Controls floor access and amendment structure, enabling leadership to gatekeep fiscal packages and brinkmanship timelines.

DPS-03.name: House Committee on Appropriations
DPS-03.description: Writes spending bills and riders that set funding conditions, enforcement constraints, and shutdown leverage points.

DPS-03.name: House Freedom Caucus
DPS-03.description: Acts as a disciplined bloc that can withhold votes to force concessions on spending cuts, riders, and procedural demands.

DPS-03.name: Senate Committee on Appropriations
DPS-03.description: Negotiates toplines and omnibus terms, shaping final fiscal outcomes and cross-chamber bargaining constraints.

DPS-03.name: Senate Republican Conference
DPS-03.description: Coordinates holds, confirmations strategy, and debt-ceiling/shutdown bargaining posture to extract policy and procedural concessions.

DPS-04.name: NVIDIA
DPS-04.description: Dominates the accelerator stack and software ecosystem that determines frontier training capacity and deployment economics.

DPS-04.name: OpenAI
DPS-04.description: Pushes frontier model releases and platform distribution while shaping safety/regulatory proposals around foundation model governance.

DPS-04.name: Google DeepMind
DPS-04.description: Competes at the model frontier and influences compute, research publication norms, and policy positioning via major releases and benchmarks.

DPS-04.name: Microsoft Azure AI / Azure Data Center Operations
DPS-04.description: Scales sovereign and enterprise AI deployment through hyperscale data centers, model hosting, and capital commitments for compute.

DPS-04.name: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
DPS-04.description: Expands AI infrastructure and custom silicon/data-center footprint to control model training and inference capacity at scale.

DPS-05.name: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
DPS-05.description: Sets monetary policy and bank supervision posture that collides or aligns with fiscal expansion, market stability, and political pressure.

DPS-05.name: U.S. Department of the Treasury
DPS-05.description: Manages issuance strategy and cash management that interacts with deficits, rates, and market plumbing under regime stress.

DPS-05.name: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
DPS-05.description: Defines key boundaries for crypto/market structure enforcement that affects financial deregulation and capital formation narratives.

DPS-05.name: Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC)
DPS-05.description: Competes for jurisdiction over crypto derivatives and market oversight, shaping the regulatory perimeter of new financial rails.

DPS-05.name: Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs
DPS-05.description: Controls confirmation and legislative chokepoints for banking/market rules that mediate Fed independence and deregulation agendas.

DPS-06.name: U.S. Department of Defense — Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition & Sustainment (OUSD(A&S))
DPS-06.description: Rewrites procurement incentives and acquisition pathways that determine whether software-first and autonomy-centric firms displace legacy primes.

DPS-06.name: Defense Innovation Unit (DIU)

DPS-06.name: Palantir Technologies
DPS-06.description: Expands data-integration and operational AI platforms across defense customers, anchoring the software-defined warfare stack.

DPS-06.name: Anduril Industries

DPS-06.name: U.S. Air Force — Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO)
DPS-06.description: Uses fast-track authorities and classified programs to shift spend toward novel platforms, autonomy, and accelerated fielding.

DPS-07.name: X (formerly Twitter)
DPS-07.description: Sets real-time political information flow via algorithmic amplification, policy enforcement, and creator ecosystem incentives.

DPS-07.name: Meta (Facebook/Instagram)
DPS-07.description: Shapes mass-scale distribution through ranking systems, political ad policies, and content moderation decisions affecting reach and legitimacy narratives.

DPS-07.name: YouTube (Google)
DPS-07.description: Controls recommendation and monetization systems that determine which political commentators and outlets gain sustained audience dominance.

DPS-07.name: Fox News Media
DPS-07.description: Coordinates high-discipline partisan agenda-setting and narrative repetition that feeds back into elite cues and voter priors.

DPS-07.name: Turning Point USA
DPS-07.description: Runs a youth/creator mobilization and messaging machine that converts online attention into coordinated narrative campaigns and offline action.

DPS-08.name: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)
DPS-08.description: Determines key transmission, interconnection, and market-rule outcomes that govern grid buildout pace under surging load growth.

DPS-08.name: U.S. Department of the Interior — Bureau of Land Management (BLM)
DPS-08.description: Controls permitting and right-of-way decisions on federal lands that can accelerate or block fossil, renewables, and transmission projects.

DPS-08.name: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers

DPS-08.name: U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC)

DPS-08.name: American Petroleum Institute (API)
DPS-08.description: Coordinates industry lobbying and regulatory strategy to expand fossil infrastructure permitting and constrain climate-rule enforcement.

DPS-09.name: U.S. Department of Defense — Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office (CDAO)

DPS-09.name: Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)

DPS-09.name: Ballard Partners

DPS-06.description: The dominant prime contractor replacing legacy aerospace with reusable launch capacity and proliferated LEO satellite architectures.

DPS-06.name: Palantir Technologies
DPS-06.description: The software prime embedding operating systems for decision-making and targeting across combatant commands.

DPS-06.name: Anduril Industries
DPS-06.description: The defense manufacturer driving the shift toward autonomous systems, loitering munitions, and software-defined hardware.

DPS-06.name: Defense Innovation Unit (DIU)
DPS-06.description: The Pentagon's internal bridge organization accelerating the adoption of commercial technology over traditional acquisition cycles.

DPS-07.name: X Corp (Twitter)
DPS-07.description: The central algorithmic platform reshaping real-time political narrative flow through ownership-directed amplification and moderation.

DPS-07.name: Fox News Media
DPS-07.description: The legacy cable dominance mechanism coordinating conservative talking points and validating alternative information ecosystems.

DPS-07.name: Turning Point USA
DPS-07.description: The influencer and campus mobilization network translating online engagement into physical activist infrastructure.

DPS-07.name: Meta (Global Affairs & Policy)
DPS-07.description: The algorithmic gatekeeper modulating the reach of political content across Instagram and Facebook in response to regulatory pressure.

DPS-08.name: American Petroleum Institute (API)
DPS-08.description: The unified fossil fuel lobby drafting executive orders to maximize extraction and dismantle climate reporting mandates.

DPS-08.name: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)
DPS-08.description: The critical chokepoint agency adjudicating grid interconnection, pipeline approvals, and regional transmission planning.

DPS-08.name: Constellation Energy
DPS-08.description: The nuclear operator representing the baseload power resurgence driven by hyperscaler data center energy demands.

DPS-08.name: Earthjustice
DPS-08.description: The legal coalition executing the opposition strategy of permit challenges and environmental impact litigation.

DPS-09.name: Founders Fund
DPS-09.description: The venture capital node linking Silicon Valley libertarian ideology directly to defense and administrative state reform agendas.

DPS-09.name: America PAC
DPS-09.description: The billionaire-funded political vehicle operationalizing high-net-worth capital into ground-game infrastructure and policy influence.

DPS-09.name: Ballard Partners
DPS-09.description: The influential lobbying firm acting as the transactional bridge between corporate interests and the inner circle of the executive.

DPS-09.name: Oracle Corporation
DPS-09.description: The enterprise tech giant leveraging close executive alignment to secure cloud contracts and influence database governance.

DPS-10.name: Elias Law Group
DPS-10.description: The primary legal engine coordinating the Democratic response to redistricting, voting rules, and election certification challenges.

DPS-10.name: RNC Election Integrity Division
DPS-10.description: The institutionalized GOP operation deploying lawyers and poll watchers to challenge voter rolls and election administration procedures.

DPS-10.name: American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU)
DPS-10.description: The civil society defender preparing the 'firewall' of litigation against mass deportation and civil rights infringements.

DPS-10.name: Democracy Alliance
DPS-10.description: The donor coordination hub directing progressive capital toward long-term institutional rebuilding and state-level infrastructure.

gaps: DPS-01: Missing core White House/EOP control nodes you included (White House Office/Chief of Staff, White House Counsel, OMB as an institution beyond just 'DOGE', and especially OMB-OIRA as the regulatory choke point).
DPS-01: Missing U.S. Office of Special Counsel (OSC) as a civil-service discipline/whistleblower pathway node (present in your list, absent in A/B/C).
DPS-01: Missing DOJ Office of the Attorney General as the executive-branch legal/enforcement posture setter (A/B/C mention House Judiciary/Judicial Watch/AFL but not DOJ leadership as an assembly).
DPS-02: Missing Alliance Defending Freedom (ADF) as a major strategic-litigation engine constraining the administrative state (present in your list, absent in A/B/C).
DPS-03: Missing House Committee on Rules as the floor-access gatekeeper for fiscal brinkmanship (present in your list, absent in A/B/C).
DPS-03: Missing Senate Committee on Appropriations as a distinct assembly (present in your list; A/B/C mostly collapse to leadership/conference or omit).

DPS-04: Missing Microsoft Azure AI / Azure Data Center Operations and AWS as infrastructure operators (A/B/C mention Microsoft/AWS partnerships but not the data-center/compute-operations layer as a trackable assembly; your list makes it explicit).
DPS-05: Missing SEC and CFTC as the key market-structure/crypto perimeter definers (present in your list; A/B/C largely substitute 'Coinbase/crypto lobby' or generic finance actors).
DPS-06: Missing DoD OUSD(A&S) as the procurement-rule and acquisition-incentive setter (present in your list; A/B/C focus on vendors and/or SASC but not the acquisition bureaucracy).
DPS-06: Missing USAF Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO) as a fast-track spend/fielding node (present in your list; absent in A/B/C).
DPS-07: Missing YouTube as a distinct recommendation/monetization gatekeeper (present in your list; A/B/C omit or replace with generic 'podcast/influencer ecosystem').

DPS-08: Missing Bureau of Land Management (BLM), U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, and NRC as the permitting/licensing bottlenecks (present in your list; A/B/C mostly swap in industry groups/committees or omit permitting agencies).
DPS-09: Missing GSA Federal Acquisition Service as the government-wide procurement gateway (present in your list; absent in A/B/C).
DPS-09: Missing DoD CDAO as the AI demand-signal/standards node (present in your list; absent in A/B/C).
DPS-09: Missing OSTP as the White House tech-policy convening/coordination node (present in your list; absent in A/B/C).
DPS-09: Missing Business Roundtable as a coordinated corporate policy/spending channel (present in your list; absent in A/B/C).
DPS-10: Missing Election Assistance Commission (EAC) as the federal election-support/standards focal point (present in your list; absent in A/B/C).
DPS-10: Missing DNC as a national counter-mobilization/coordination node (present in your list; A includes DNC, but B/C omit).
redundancies: Judicial Crisis Network and Judicial Crisis Network / The Concord Fund are the same network/branding, appears in DPS-02 (Model A) and DPS-02 (Model C).
X (formerly Twitter), X Corp, and X (Twitter) refer to the same organization, appears in DPS-07 (your list/Model A/Model B/Model C) and also as part of broader Musk clusters in DPS-09 (Model A).
America First Legal Foundation and America First Legal are the same organization, appears in DPS-02 (Model A) and DPS-01 (Model A/Model B).
Judicial Watch / Tom Fitton FOIA Operation and Judicial Watch are the same organization, appears in DPS-01 (Model A) and DPS-01 (Model B).
Democracy Docket and Marc Elias / Democracy Docket Litigation Network refer to the same core org/network, appears in DPS-10 (your list/Model A) and DPS-10 (Model A).

Elias Law Group and Marc Elias / Democracy Docket Litigation Network are overlapping entities under Marc Elias (distinct orgs but functionally the same litigation hub), appears in DPS-10 (Model B) and DPS-10 (Model A).
Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies and Federalist Society are the same organization, appears in DPS-02 (your list/Model A/Model B/Model C).
American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) and ACLU are the same organization, appears in DPS-10 (your list/Model A/Model B).
Turning Point USA and Turning Point USA / Kirk–Rufo Conservative Media Network are the same core org with an expanded descriptor, appears in DPS-07 (your list/Model A/Model B/Model C).

Fox News Media, Fox News / Murdoch Media Empire, and Fox Corporation are the same corporate family/asset cluster (not identical legal entities but effectively the same assembly for tracking), appears in DPS-07 (your list/Model A/Model B/Model C).
vague_entries: DPS-01 (Model A): "Heritage Foundation / Project 2025 Implementation Network" is too broad — suggest: Heritage Foundation (Project 2025) + specific implementation nodes (e.g., Presidential Personnel Office staffing pipeline; OMB/OIRA regulatory review targets; agency landing teams).

DPS-02 (Model A): "State Attorney General Republican Alliance (RAGA litigation coalition)" is too broad — suggest: identify specific high-impact AG offices (e.g., Texas AG, Florida AG) or the RAGA entity itself plus named multi-state suit coordinators.
DPS-03 (Model A): "House Republican Leadership" is too broad — suggest: Speaker’s Office + House Rules Committee + House Appropriations leadership as separate trackable nodes.

DPS-03 (Model A): "Senate Republican Conference Leadership" is too broad — suggest: Senate GOP Conference (as an org) plus specific procedural chokepoints (e.g., holds/whip operation) or relevant committees (Appropriations).
DPS-04 (Model C): "Microsoft Cloud & AI Group" is too broad — suggest: Azure AI + Azure Data Center Operations (compute buildout, procurement, and hosting) as separable assemblies.

DPS-05 (Model A): "Macro Hedge Fund / Private Equity Nexus (Citadel, Apollo, Druckenmiller)" is too broad — suggest: pick 1–2 specific firms (e.g., Citadel Securities vs Citadel LLC; Apollo) or a specific trade association/lobby vehicle.

DPS-07 (Model A): "Podcast–Influencer Ecosystem (Joe Rogan / Tucker Carlson Network)" is too broad — suggest: Spotify (distribution for Rogan) + Tucker Carlson Media (company) + YouTube/Rumble as distribution nodes.

DPS-08 (Model A): "Hyperscaler Energy Procurement Bloc (Amazon / Google / Microsoft / Meta)" is too broad — suggest: track each firm’s energy procurement unit or a concrete coalition/vehicle (e.g., specific PPAs, data-center siting teams, or named industry groups).
DPS-09 (Model A): "Thiel Network" is too broad — suggest: Founders Fund + Palantir + specific PAC/advocacy vehicles (if any) as separate assemblies.

DPS-09 (Model A): "Musk Enterprise Cluster" is too broad — suggest: SpaceX (Starshield) + Tesla + X + xAI as separate assemblies (and only include 'DOGE role' if it is a defined government entity with a stable org chart).
DPS-09 (Model A): "Blackstone / Schwarzman Political-Capital Exchange" is too broad — suggest: Blackstone Inc. (government relations) or a specific PAC/association channel.

DPS-10 (Model A): "Arabella Advisors / Soros-backed Civic Engagement Network" is too broad — suggest: Arabella Advisors (as manager) plus one or two specific funds (e.g., Sixteen Thirty Fund) as separate trackable entities.
DPS-10 (Model A): "ACLU / Democracy Defense Legal Coalition" is too broad — suggest: ACLU (national) + specific coalition name(s) with membership/lead counsel identified.

DPS-08 (Model B): "Kinder Morgan" is underspecified for the permitting/regime-competition frame — suggest: Kinder Morgan Government Affairs + FERC pipeline certification docket activity (or replace with FERC/Corps/BLM/NRC permitting nodes).

DPS-05 (Model B): "Americans for Tax Reform" is generic relative to monetary-fiscal-financial plumbing — suggest: add/replace with SEC, CFTC, primary dealer/bond-market plumbing nodes, or specific crypto trade associations if the intent is the regulatory perimeter.

Americans for Tax Reform for anti-tax mobilization in DPS-05 fiscal tension, missing from all other models

SpaceX / Starshield appears in DPS-06 and as part of Musk Enterprise Cluster in DPS-09 (Model A)
X Corp appears in DPS-07 and as part of Musk Enterprise Cluster in DPS-09 (Model A)
vague_entries: Macro Hedge Fund / Private Equity Nexus (Citadel, Apollo, Druckenmiller) in Model A DPS-05 too broad — suggest: Citadel Securities, Apollo Global Management
Hyperscaler Energy Procurement Bloc (Amazon / Google / Microsoft / Meta) in Model A DPS-08 too vague — suggest: Microsoft Corporation, Amazon.com, Inc.
Thiel Network (Founders Fund / Palantir / Anduril / allied ventures) in Model A DPS-09 too broad — suggest: Founders Fund, Palantir Technologies
Podcast–Influencer Ecosystem (Joe Rogan / Tucker Carlson Network) in Model A DPS-07 too vague — suggest: Tucker Carlson Network
Democracy Defense Legal Coalition in Model A DPS-10 too generic — suggest: Brennan Center for Justice, ACLU

gaps: Presidential Personnel Office (PPO) is missing from Models A and C in DPS-01; this is the specific mechanism for Schedule F implementation and loyalty testing, distinct from external advisory groups like DOGE or Heritage.

Private Credit / Shadow Banking sector (e.g., Apollo, Blackstone) is missing from Models B and C in DPS-05; this sector is a critical rival to traditional banking regulation and a key driver of the new financial regime.

Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is missing from Models A and C in DPS-06; this is the essential internal bridge organization connecting Silicon Valley primes (Anduril/Palantir) to the Pentagon bureaucracy.
Crypto-specific lobbying infrastructure (e.g., Coinbase, Stand With Crypto) is missing from Models B and C in DPS-05; this is a distinct power center from traditional finance or tech.
redundancies: Senate/House Committees (Appropriations, Banking, Armed Services) appear in DPS-03 and again in DPS-05, DPS-06, and DPS-08 across all models; while functional, listing the same committee twice dilutes the distinction between 'Legislative Gatekeeping' (DPS-03) and specific policy domains.
America First Legal (DPS-02) and Stephen Miller Policy Shop (DPS-01) in Model A represent the same operational node/personnel cluster.
Heritage Foundation and Project 2025 (Model A, DPS-01) are listed as distinct elements but function as a single organizational entity for this purpose.
vague_entries: "Thiel Network" and "Musk Enterprise Cluster" in Model A (DPS-09) are too broad; these are networks of influence, not specific organizational assemblies (suggest: Founders Fund, America PAC).
"Hyperscaler Energy Procurement Bloc" in Model A (DPS-08) is a concept, not an organization (suggest: specific entity like Constellation Energy or a specific hyperscaler energy subsidiary).
"Podcast–Influencer Ecosystem" in Model A (DPS-07) is too amorphous to track (suggest: specific media companies like Spotify or Tucker Carlson Network).

"Business Roundtable" in Model B (DPS-09) is too generic of a trade association to capture the specific "Techno-Oligarchic" alignment (suggest: specific VC firms or lobbying groups like Ballard Partners).

cell_assemblies.name: Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)
cell_assemblies.description: Musk-led White House advisory entity that executes agency workforce reductions, IT system audits, and spending freezes to centralize executive control over the federal bureaucracy.

cell_assemblies.name: White House Office of Presidential Personnel (PPO)
cell_assemblies.description: The internal executive mechanism that enforces loyalty tests, manages Schedule F reclassifications, and coordinates the replacement of career civil servants with political appointees across federal agencies.

cell_assemblies.name: Office of Management and Budget (OMB) — including OIRA
cell_assemblies.description: The executive control center that uses regulatory review, budget apportionment, guidance documents, and impoundment authority to steer or stall agency rulemaking and consolidate White House direction over the administrative state.

cell_assemblies.name: Department of Justice — Office of the Attorney General
cell_assemblies.description: Shapes selective enforcement posture and legal strategy across the executive branch through prosecutorial discretion, OLC opinions, and litigation decisions that enable or constrain executive power consolidation.

cell_assemblies.name: Heritage Foundation (Project 2025)
cell_assemblies.description: Kevin Roberts's policy-and-personnel pipeline that pre-vetted political appointees, supplies agency-level playbooks, and provides the ideological backbone for administrative-state dismantlement.

cell_assemblies.name: Supreme Court of the United States (Conservative Supermajority)
cell_assemblies.description: The six-justice bloc (Thomas-Alito-Gorsuch-Kavanaugh-Barrett-Roberts) that issues major-questions, non-delegation, and Chevron-dismantling rulings structurally transferring regulatory power from agencies to courts and Congress.

cell_assemblies.name: Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies
cell_assemblies.description: The intellectual and networking pipeline that selects, vets, and coordinates the placement of conservative judicial nominees committed to constraining administrative-state authority.

cell_assemblies.name: Judicial Crisis Network / The Concord Fund
cell_assemblies.description: The Leo-network dark-money coordination hub that finances judicial confirmation campaigns, funds strategic litigation vehicles, and coordinates messaging to protect and extend the conservative court majority.

cell_assemblies.name: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit
cell_assemblies.description: The high-impact appellate venue that issues nationwide injunctions against federal agency regulations and generates the precedents that tee up Supreme Court review of administrative-state doctrines.

cell_assemblies.name: America First Legal Foundation
cell_assemblies.description: Stephen Miller's litigation arm that files targeted lawsuits against administrative-state regulations and civil-service protections, serving as a rapid-deployment complement to state AG challenges and enabling executive restructuring.

cell_assemblies.name: House Freedom Caucus
cell_assemblies.description: The hard-right House bloc that uses shutdown threats, procedural hostage-taking, and vote withholding to extract deeper spending cuts, policy riders, and concessions from leadership on every must-pass fiscal vehicle.

cell_assemblies.name: House Committee on Appropriations
cell_assemblies.description: The battleground committee where specific agency funding lines are drafted, cut, or leveraged during shutdown negotiations and continuing-resolution brinkmanship.

cell_assemblies.name: House Committee on Ways and Means
cell_assemblies.description: The tax-writing committee that drafts reconciliation bills, structures tariff authorities, and controls the revenue side of every fiscal-brinkmanship negotiation.

cell_assemblies.name: Senate Republican Conference Leadership
cell_assemblies.description: The Thune-Barrasso upper-chamber steering mechanism that manages floor procedure, whips 60-vote thresholds, sequences confirmations, and negotiates bipartisan deals on debt ceilings and appropriations.

cell_assemblies.name: Senate Democratic Caucus Leadership
cell_assemblies.description: The Schumer-Durbin caucus that uses filibuster leverage, amendment votes, and confirmation holds to extract concessions, block riders, and force bipartisan negotiations on fiscal deadlines.

cell_assemblies.name: NVIDIA Corporation
cell_assemblies.description: Jensen Huang's GPU monopoly that sets the clock speed of the entire AI race by controlling the design, allocation, and pricing of H100/B200 training and inference accelerators.

cell_assemblies.name: OpenAI
cell_assemblies.description: The leading frontier AI laboratory that pushes foundation-model capabilities (GPT series), drives capital-intensive compute scaling, and shapes safety and regulatory proposals around model governance.

cell_assemblies.name: Microsoft — Azure AI & Cloud Infrastructure
cell_assemblies.description: The hyperscale infrastructure entity that integrates OpenAI model weights into global enterprise and government software stacks via Azure data centers, custom silicon, and sovereign-cloud deployments.

cell_assemblies.name: Google DeepMind / Alphabet AI Division
cell_assemblies.description: The merged Pichai-Hassabis AI unit that controls Gemini model development, TPU chip design, Google Cloud distribution, and the world's largest proprietary training-data corpus.

cell_assemblies.name: Anthropic
cell_assemblies.description: The Amodei siblings' safety-oriented frontier lab backed by Amazon's multi-billion-dollar investment and AWS distribution, positioning as the enterprise-trust alternative to OpenAI.

cell_assemblies.name: Federal Reserve Board of Governors / FOMC
cell_assemblies.description: The Powell-led monetary authority whose rate decisions, balance-sheet management, and bank supervision set the baseline interest-rate regime against which all fiscal, financial, and political actors must operate.

cell_assemblies.name: U.S. Department of the Treasury
cell_assemblies.description: Scott Bessent's Treasury manages debt issuance strategy, coupon-vs-bill mix, tariff revenue flows, and sanctions policy, serving as the executive branch's primary interface with bond markets.

cell_assemblies.name: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
cell_assemblies.description: Defines key boundaries for crypto and market-structure enforcement that affect financial deregulation, capital formation narratives, and the regulatory perimeter of new financial rails.

cell_assemblies.name: Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs
cell_assemblies.description: The committee that controls confirmation and legislative chokepoints for banking and market rules, mediating Fed independence, stablecoin frameworks, and deregulation agendas.

cell_assemblies.name: Coinbase Global / Stand With Crypto Coalition
cell_assemblies.description: Brian Armstrong-led industry coalition that funds congressional campaigns, drafts stablecoin and market-structure bills, and pushes for CFTC-over-SEC jurisdiction to integrate crypto into the formal financial regime.

cell_assemblies.name: Palantir Technologies
cell_assemblies.description: Alex Karp's data-integration and AI platform that embeds across Army, intelligence community, and combatant commands, positioning software-defined C2 as the new center of defense procurement.

cell_assemblies.name: Anduril Industries
cell_assemblies.description: Palmer Luckey and Trae Stephens's autonomy-and-software defense firm that builds Lattice, counter-drone systems, and autonomous vehicles, serving as the flagship disruptor of legacy prime contracting.

cell_assemblies.name: SpaceX — Starshield Division
cell_assemblies.description: Musk's launch and satellite-communications monopoly whose Starshield division provides classified space services, making SpaceX a de facto space-domain prime contractor rivaling Lockheed and Northrop.

cell_assemblies.name: Defense Innovation Unit (DIU)
cell_assemblies.description: The Pentagon's internal bridge organization that translates commercial autonomy, AI, and software capabilities into DoD contracts and scaling pathways, accelerating nontraditional vendor adoption.

cell_assemblies.name: Senate Armed Services Committee
cell_assemblies.description: The bipartisan committee (Wicker/Reed) that writes the NDAA, sets procurement authorization levels, and decides whether acquisition reform language favors new entrants or protects legacy prime workshare.

cell_assemblies.name: X Corp (formerly Twitter)
cell_assemblies.description: Musk's ownership of X gives him direct control over algorithmic amplification, content-moderation policy, and political speech norms for the platform most used by journalists and political actors.

cell_assemblies.name: Fox News Media
cell_assemblies.description: The Murdoch family's dominant right-of-center broadcast ecosystem that sets daily narrative frames for the Republican base through high-discipline partisan agenda-setting and repetition.

cell_assemblies.name: Turning Point USA
cell_assemblies.description: Charlie Kirk's youth-and-digital mobilization network that manufactures and distributes culture-war narratives across social media and conservative outlets, translating online engagement into activist infrastructure.

cell_assemblies.name: Meta Platforms — Global Affairs & Policy Division
cell_assemblies.description: Zuckerberg's platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Threads) control the largest algorithmic news-distribution surface in the US; content-moderation and ranking-system shifts directly reshape political information flow.

cell_assemblies.name: YouTube (Google)
cell_assemblies.description: Controls recommendation algorithms and monetization systems that determine which political commentators and outlets gain sustained audience dominance, functioning as a distinct narrative-distribution gatekeeper.

cell_assemblies.name: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)
cell_assemblies.description: The critical chokepoint agency adjudicating grid interconnection queues, pipeline certifications, regional transmission planning, and market rules that govern buildout pace under surging AI and electrification load.

cell_assemblies.name: American Petroleum Institute (API)
cell_assemblies.description: The unified fossil-fuel trade association that coordinates industry lobbying for accelerated drilling permits, LNG export approvals, rollback of EPA emissions rules, and executive-order drafting.

cell_assemblies.name: Senate Committee on Energy and Natural Resources
cell_assemblies.description: The Barrasso-Murkowski committee that writes permitting-reform legislation, controls federal land leasing policy, and negotiates bipartisan deals determining grid-buildout and energy-project timelines.

cell_assemblies.name: Constellation Energy
cell_assemblies.description: The nuclear fleet operator representing the baseload power resurgence driven by hyperscaler data-center energy demands, executing PPAs that signal the viability of nuclear revival at scale.

cell_assemblies.name: Earthjustice
cell_assemblies.description: The environmental legal coalition that uses NEPA and Clean Air Act litigation to block or delay fossil-fuel permits, force environmental review of data-center power demand, and constrain permitting acceleration.

cell_assemblies.name: Founders Fund
cell_assemblies.description: Peter Thiel's venture capital node that links Silicon Valley libertarian ideology directly to defense, intelligence, and administrative-state reform agendas through portfolio companies and political patronage.

cell_assemblies.name: Ballard Partners
cell_assemblies.description: Brian Ballard's lobbying firm that serves as the primary transactional bridge between Silicon Valley executives seeking White House access and the Trump political operation seeking donor capital and platform cooperation.

cell_assemblies.name: Oracle Corporation
cell_assemblies.description: Larry Ellison's enterprise tech giant that leverages a close personal relationship with Trump to secure major government cloud contracts (CIA, DoD, TikTok deal structure) and AI infrastructure partnerships.

cell_assemblies.name: America PAC
cell_assemblies.description: The billionaire-funded political vehicle that operationalizes high-net-worth tech capital into ground-game infrastructure, voter contact operations, and direct policy influence on behalf of the governing coalition.

cell_assemblies.name: Elias Law Group / Democracy Docket
cell_assemblies.description: Marc Elias's dedicated election-law firm and media platform that coordinates pre-election ballot-access litigation, post-election recount strategy, and real-time legal rapid-response across all 50 states.

cell_assemblies.name: American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU)
cell_assemblies.description: Anthony Romero's civil-liberties organization that anchors voting-rights litigation, challenges voter-roll purges and ID laws, and prepares firewall litigation against civil-rights infringements in election administration.

cell_assemblies.name: Honest Elections Project / Cleta Mitchell Election Integrity Network
cell_assemblies.description: The Leo-funded Honest Elections Project and Mitchell's volunteer-training network that installs poll watchers, challenges voter registrations, and builds legal and procedural infrastructure for contesting election results.

cell_assemblies.name: Republican National Committee — Election Integrity Division
cell_assemblies.description: The institutionalized GOP operation deploying lawyers, poll watchers, and post-election dispute infrastructure to challenge voter rolls, contest election administration procedures, and coordinate ballot-access strategy across states.

cell_assemblies.name: Arabella Advisors / Sixteen Thirty Fund
cell_assemblies.description: The progressive dark-money fiscal-sponsorship network that funds voter-registration drives, ballot-initiative campaigns, and counter-mobilization infrastructure through its constellation of pass-through entities.

cell_assemblies.name: House Committee on the Judiciary (Majority)
cell_assemblies.description: Jim Jordan's committee that provides legislative air cover for executive consolidation through investigations justifying firings and restructuring, while blocking oversight of White House power expansion.
